data.name: checkBox_legalnoticetext.ToolTip
data.value:
Windows Login Message
LegalNoticeCaption and LegalNoticeText are policy keys used to display a custom message when a user logs into Windows. They are typically used in corporate or organizational environments to show legal notices, security warnings, or terms of use.
When a user attempts to log into Windows (before entering their password), the system will forcibly display a message window with this content. The user must click the OK button before proceeding with the login process.

data.name: checkBox_legalnoticecaption.ToolTip
data.value:
Windows Login Message
LegalNoticeCaption and LegalNoticeText are policy keys used to display a custom message when a user logs into Windows. They are typically used in corporate or organizational environments to show legal notices, security warnings, or terms of use.
When a user attempts to log into Windows (before entering their password), the system will forcibly display a message window with this content. The user must click the OK button before proceeding with the login process.

data.name: label_registeredOrganization.ToolTip
data.value:
RegisteredOrganization is a key value used to record the registered organization of a Windows system. It identifies the "organization" or "company" name that this Windows computer belongs to, typically used in corporate, school, or institutional environments. In Windows, this information appears in the following locations: · The "Registered to" area in System Properties (sysdm.cpl), located below the owner name · The bottom of the About Windows dialog (winver) · The licensing information of certain enterprise software
When users view basic system information, this field clearly indicates the organization that owns this Windows copy. If left blank, only the owner information will be displayed.

data.name: label_registeredOwner.ToolTip
data.value:
RegisteredOwner is a key value used to record the registered owner of a Windows system. It identifies who the "owner" of this Windows computer is — typically a personal name or username. In Windows, this information appears in the following locations: · The "Registered to" area in System Properties (sysdm.cpl) · The bottom of the About Windows dialog (winver) · Installation information or the Help menu of certain software
When users view basic system information, this field clearly indicates the ownership identity of this Windows copy.

data.name: label_PromptOnSecureDesktop.Text
data.value: Show UAC Prompts on Secure Desktop

data.name: checkBox_NoSetTaskbar.Text
data.value: Disable Taskbar Disable Start Menu Properties

data.name: checkBox_NoPrinterTabs.Text
data.value: Disable "General" and "Details" Tabs in Printer Properties

data.name: checkBox_ClearRecentDocsonExit.Text
data.value: Auto-Clear Run & Search History on Exit

data.name: checkBox_EditLevel.Text
data.value: Disable Programs Separator in Start Menu

data.name: button_RegWinDefender_NoSystem.Text
data.value: To use this feature with administrator privileges, please first disable Windows Defender antivirus/anti-spyware features in Windows Security Center settings, or add this software to the whitelist. Otherwise, this feature may be blocked and fail to work properly.

data.name: checkBox_WinDefender_DisableScanOnRealtimeEnable.Text
data.value: Disable Initial Scan on Real-Time Enable

data.name: checkBox_WinDefender_DisableRoutinelyTakingAction.Text
data.value: Block Defender from Auto-Acting on Threats

data.name: label12.Text
data.value: Note: This plan offers negligible performance improvement but may increase hardware wear. Recommended for servers and workstations only.
